# terrible_cisco_intersight_intersight_syslog_policy (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) The name assigned to the Syslog Policy.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) The user-defined description for the Syslog Policy.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `first_remote_logging_enabled` (Boolean) If `true`, enables the first remote syslog server destination.
- `first_remote_logging_hostname` (String) Hostname or IP Address of the first syslog server where log should be stored. This parameter is required if `first_remote_logging_enabled` is `true`.
- `first_remote_logging_minimum_severity` (String) Lowest level of messages to be included in the first remote log.
- `first_remote_logging_port` (Number) Port number used for logging on first syslog server.
- `first_remote_logging_protocol` (String) Transport layer protocol for transmission of log messages to first syslog server.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `local_logging_minimum_severity` (String) Lowest level of messages to be included in the local log.
- `organization` (String) The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `second_remote_logging_enabled` (Boolean) If `true`, enables the second remote syslog server destination.
- `second_remote_logging_hostname` (String) Hostname or IP Address of the second syslog server where log should be stored. This parameter is required if `second_remote_logging_enabled` is `true`.
- `second_remote_logging_minimum_severity` (String) Lowest level of messages to be included in the second remote log.
- `second_remote_logging_port` (Number) Port number used for logging on second syslog server.
- `second_remote_logging_protocol` (String) Transport layer protocol for transmission of log messages to second syslog server.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) If `present`, will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If `absent`, will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `api_response` (String) The API response output returned by the specified resource.
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
